pure_fr.evidence: Output A defines a single atomic, solution-neutral FR focused on enabling cycle execution within a target time, with clear testability via telemetry and sub-FR decomposition to maintain atomicity. Output B treats cycle time as a system-level KPI with a less atomic FR that mixes performance outcome and monitoring, requiring further decomposition to achieve atomicity and solution neutrality.

fr_dp_bridge.evidence: Output A demonstrates strong adherence to the Independence Axiom by integrating DP1, DP2, and DP3 within a cohesive Vehicle module boundary, minimizing diffusion and coupling with well-defined external interfaces. It applies the Information Axiom by avoiding unnecessary complexity through integrated control and performance management, with clear mitigation of coupling risks via abstraction layers and telemetry feedback. Output B correctly identifies the system-level nature of the KPI and decomposes cycle time budgets across subsystems, but exhibits higher coupling risk due to distributed ownership and requires further refinement of integration responsibilities and budget allocation, leading to moderate complexity and coupling.

dp_im_bridge.evidence: Output A demonstrates excellent containment of all three DPs (timing budgets, state machine, powertrain sizing) within a single cohesive Vehicle module with minimal diffusion (0.08), supported by explicit mitigation strategies and clear interface boundaries. Output B assigns cycle time KPI ownership to the NGHE Solution system-level module, which appropriately integrates multiple subsystems but inherently diffuses DP logic across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services, requiring further decomposition and governance to avoid diffusion risks.

pure_im.evidence: Output A provides detailed structural modularity evidence with high containment (0.92) and low diffusion (0.08) scores for the Vehicle module, supported by explicit interface definitions and risk mitigations, demonstrating strong cohesion and stable ownership boundaries. Output B also shows high-level system modularity with NGHE Solution as the integration boundary and good containment (95) but with some diffusion risks due to distributed ownership across subsystems and less detailed module-level clustering evidence.

fr_im_bridge.evidence: Output A clearly allocates the entire FR to a single cohesive Vehicle module with well-defined external interfaces and strong verification feasibility via telemetry, enabling direct measurement of cycle time KPIs and explicit ownership of timing budgets and safety constraints. Output B assigns FR ownership at the system level (NGHE Solution) with clear integration responsibilities and budget allocation across subsystems, but verification and ownership clarity depend on further decomposition and creation of a dedicated integration team, introducing some diffusion risk and requiring additional governance.
